133708.explanation: The current north-south connection runs through Doorn. That is the place to transfer.

133709.explanation: The N229 is a provincial road outside our municipality.

133711.explanation: After the latest adjustment of the Legal Debt Restructuring for Natural Persons, the situation for families with debts has been sufficiently brought under control within a reasonable timeframe.

133712.explanation: The municipality has always taken this into account in its contracting policy, and that must continue.

133713.explanation: Children who need help should not be hindered by rules. But in general, we must move towards a society where children are not labeled, but where help is focused on their entire social environment, close by and without many barriers.

133714.explanation: Supporting relationships that have hit hard times is valuable given the harm to children and the societal costs of a divorce.

133651.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports citizen assemblies: residents who are randomly selected for this assembly deliberate on the issue. All interests are considered, including those of home seekers and future generations.

133652.explanation: We must get rid of fossil fuels as soon as possible. Our municipality can take the lead in this, for example through procurement.

133653.explanation: Give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, and wonderful things will happen. Our municipality should support this, including financially.

133654.explanation: Our elected municipal council manages the budget for all villages; they ensure that villages receive what they need.

133655.explanation: Young people are our future. GroenLinks-PvdA believes they should be actively involved in politics: their voice should be reflected in decision-making.

133656.explanation: We want to maintain public facilities because they benefit everyone (e.g. road maintenance, community centers, libraries, social services). Income is under pressure. To prevent cuts to these facilities, our municipality can consider the property tax (ozb).

133657.explanation: In today’s world, shopkeepers decide for themselves. GroenLinks-PvdA does not see a need to extend current opening hours.

133658.explanation: This will help us achieve cleaner air, reduce nitrogen emissions, and create a natural environment with more space for biodiversity.

133659.explanation: The central government and the Province provide this budget.

133660.explanation: This is urgently needed to protect our natural environment and solve nitrogen problems.

133661.explanation: For our climate, we must make every effort to ensure the energy transition succeeds. Besides saving energy, more sustainable, clean energy is needed, such as solar panels on roofs and land. Preferably through combined land use, like between cows, arable farming, or nature.

133662.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with wind turbines or solar panels. They use the electricity on the same site, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

133663.explanation: It’s always about people, legal or not. We must especially prevent people from ending up in this situation. For this, our municipality must engage with national agencies.

133664.explanation: Priority is given with an urgency statement, in cases of personal (medical, financial) emergency, downsizing from a large to smaller home, due to your profession (care, education, police), and also as a status holder. We also support priority for local home seekers who want to stay in their own village.

133666.explanation: A general ban is not necessary. It is, however, needed in calm weather (yellow and red code from the RIVM burning guide) because then it is dangerous to the health of nearby residents.

133667.explanation: Wind turbines provide clean energy. GroenLinks-PvdA believes wind energy should not be taboo, as was the case during the last council period.

133668.explanation: Problem wolves must be controlled. Our environment has space for wolves, but good public information remains necessary. Owners must be assisted in protecting their livestock.

133669.explanation: Our municipality can play a positive role: cooperating in realizing food forests and the local food agenda, which focuses on local and sustainable food.

133670.explanation: Small-scale, local events bring great joy to village communities, but we also welcome the Flower Parade, the fair, and larger cultural events.

133671.explanation: Recreation at Henschotermeer is welcome, but events make this area vulnerable. We want to prevent an amusement park there.

133672.explanation: If there is enough space, terraces enhance liveliness. But elderly people and wheelchair users must have clear paths.

133673.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is not in favor of a general ban because advertising opportunities can be important for local entrepreneurs or social organizations. However, we want to oppose advertising for things like gambling, flying, and the fossil fuel industry.

133674.explanation: Sports provide satisfaction and keep you healthy, but are often too expensive for low-income people. Our municipality ensures that people with low incomes can participate for free.

133675.explanation: The vitality of our residents starts with physical activity. In a water-rich country like the Netherlands, school swimming is essential. This should be facilitated with support from our municipality.

133676.explanation: Often associations request artificial grass fields because natural grass wears out quickly and can be used less intensively. GroenLinks-PvdA agrees with this, but the harmful rubber granulate must be removed from artificial fields as soon as possible.

133677.explanation: Every village needs a library for residents to meet, get language support, and access culture. Youth can escape screen time there. A library strengthens connections in a village.

133678.explanation: This requires a large investment; we must allocate our public funds to more urgent goals.

133679.explanation: Playgrounds have a vital function: for children playing and moving outside, and for neighbors to meet. It strengthens the neighborhood.

133680.explanation: Playing sports on Sundays is fine and healthy.

133681.explanation: Animal welfare is important to GroenLinks-PvdA. There is no place in our municipality for organizations that mistreat animals or threaten animal welfare. But it is going too far to ban horse riding during Playweek or to stop supporting the petting zoo.

133682.explanation: Feeling safe on the streets day and night sometimes requires camera surveillance. But only as a last resort, under strict conditions. Everyone’s privacy is highly valued. We choose to start with more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

133683.explanation: Coffeeshops are not banned in the Netherlands, so not in our municipality either. Location is very important, for example not in the middle of a residential area.

133684.explanation: Care for our natural environment and animal welfare is a good thing. A plan for people, animals, and nature in case of emergencies, such as a forest fire, is self-evident to us.

133685.explanation: The current neighborhood police officer proves useful for the community: it brings prevention, calm, and safety. Especially if connected with youth work.

133686.explanation: Everyone may run for office based on their own identity.

133687.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) already prove their usefulness; expanding their numbers helps keep our environment safe and tidy. Therefore, a budget must be allocated for this.

133688.explanation: Recreational hunting is outdated. We also have a responsibility to protect animals in our municipality, which is so rich in nature.

133691.explanation: Solving the housing shortage requires creative solutions. Providing more housing units is a high priority for GroenLinks-PvdA.

133692.explanation: This is necessary to catch up on the backlog of social rental housing in our municipality.

133693.explanation: For many locations in our municipality, six stories is already quite tall. For large construction projects like the Driebergen-Zeist station area, we can make an exception, because building higher can help address the severe housing shortage.

133694.explanation: We must put a stop to investors who buy up properties to rent them out.

133695.explanation: Too many huge distribution centers have already been built in the Netherlands. They spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by low-paid migrant workers. As far as we are concerned, no more distribution centers should be allowed. This also protects our local retailers.

133696.explanation: Building in the station area is a good plan to create many housing units. It is a well-accessible location where compact building is preferred to preserve the natural environment as much as possible.

133697.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

133698.explanation: Solving the housing shortage requires courageous decisions. We want to preserve and protect the greenery in and around our villages as much as possible. But we must also look beyond the 'red contours' to tackle the housing shortage. Moreover, this is an opportunity to build pleasant, green neighborhoods for our residents.

133699.explanation: Given the enormous need for housing in our region, we do not want to shut down our municipality.

133700.explanation: Given the large housing shortage, especially affordable homes, building outside the so-called red contours is also an option for GroenLinks-PvdA. We prefer this to come at the expense of farmland rather than nature.

133701.explanation: In our aging municipality, informal care is indispensable. If a temporary home in the backyard offers a solution, our municipality should not oppose it.

133702.explanation: Even just above the poverty line, there is much hidden misery. Help from our municipality is urgently needed there as well.

133703.explanation: Meaningful social work is beneficial, but people are not on welfare because they lack the will to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and the municipality’s trust. Extra demands only cause more stress and create new problems.

133704.explanation: Our municipality can help these people find a job, for example in the BIGA. This is a pure win-win.

133706.explanation: Reduction is not a goal in itself; livability is paramount. We prefer to make village centers car-free: relocating parking spaces to the edge of the center or village is desirable. We must keep an eye on the accessibility of village centers and shops, especially for less mobile residents.

133707.explanation: This gives more space to other road users, such as pedestrians and cyclists. This is very important for the vitality and livability of our villages and neighborhoods.

133708.explanation: The public transport connection with Amersfoort is currently inadequate. Together with the province, we are committed to affordable and frequent public transport.

133709.explanation: Fewer trucks will then pass the intersection in Doorn. So less congestion.

133711.explanation: Debt is often just one of the problems these people face. We believe everyone has the right to keep their head above water. If people can no longer manage themselves, the municipality must step in, with credit or waiving municipal taxes.

133712.explanation: It is important to be able to choose, for example, an anthroposophic GP or a 'pink' nursing home. But in acute care, such as with an ambulance, it is unacceptable to reject a caregiver based on religion, culture, or belief.

133713.explanation: To prevent problems in later life, we must provide youth with necessary care without delay, even though problems in youth care are currently large. With stricter admission criteria, youth problems are pushed further away from us.

133714.explanation: Our social village teams can offer help when families are under stress. Support can ease problems for children and relieve youth care. If a divorce turns out to be avoidable, this also reduces pressure on available housing.

statements.title: There should be a citizens' assembly about the area plan for Driebergen-Zeist station.

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

titleParts.text: komen over de gebiedsvisie van station Driebergen-Zeist.

statements.moreInfo: For the area around Driebergen‑Zeist station a plan has been drawn up with new offices and homes. That has consequences for the neighborhood around the station, such as changes to the landscape, taller buildings and existing houses that must be removed. The municipality can choose to hold a citizens' assembly: a randomly selected group of residents who can then have a say about the plans.
statements.pro: By lottery all interests are taken into account, including people who normally do not participate and future generations. A citizens’ assembly creates more and stronger support for decisions, which are then made together with residents. Citizens often know better than policy officers where problems may arise.
statements.con: A decision has already been made on the area vision for Driebergen‑Zeist. A citizens’ assembly cannot reverse that. Decisions are made by the municipal council and a citizens’ assembly should not replace that decision‑making. The existing participation policy is sufficient, and residents have already been able to have a say in the decision on this area vision.

statements.title: Shops should be closed on Sundays.
statements.moreInfo: In the Netherlands the Shop Opening Hours Act (Winkeltijdenwet) stipulates that shops may be open on weekdays and Saturdays between 06:00 and 22:00, and are in principle closed on Sundays and public holidays. Municipalities may decide themselves whether to extend opening hours, for example on Sundays.
statements.pro: For some parties it is part of their faith to have Sunday as a day of rest. Small entrepreneurs can only survive the competition if they take even less free time.
statements.con: Other parties believe shopkeepers should decide this themselves. Customers can choose when they do their shopping.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: The Housing Act (Huisvestingswet) stipulates that municipalities must provide housing to refugees with a residence permit. Municipalities may also decide which groups receive priority for a social rental home. Priority often applies to people in medical need and to those at risk of becoming homeless. Refugees who receive a residence permit must leave the asylum reception centers quickly.
statements.pro: Priority for a social rental home is given to urgent and vulnerable housing-seekers. Refugees with a residence permit are an urgent and vulnerable group; this is the way to get them out of the reception center (azc) as quickly as possible. For others, this barely increases the average waiting time for a social rental home.
statements.con: The municipality must itself determine who the most urgent and vulnerable housing applicants are. A person’s personal situation must be decisive. Prioritization increases polarization. The housing shortage is the problem; more social housing is the solution. This comes at the expense of residents who have been waiting for a home for years.

statements.title: No events should be allowed at the Henschotermeer.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.moreInfo: The Henschotermeer is a nature area with a popular lake for summer recreation. There are plans for more recreation and events around the lake, including in the evenings and in other seasons.
statements.pro: Events create waste and disturb nature and the habitat of wild animals.
statements.con: Events make the area attractive to more people.

statements.title: The municipality should ban new coffee shops.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.moreInfo: In coffeeshops you may buy soft drugs such as cannabis or hash for personal use. That is tolerated, because soft drugs are officially still illegal. There is currently one coffeeshop in the municipality, in Driebergen‑Rijsenburg.
statements.pro: New coffeeshops increase the risks of nuisance and crime. One coffeeshop in the municipality is enough. Drugs are harmful; young people and public health must be protected.
statements.con: Coffeeshops are not illegal in the Netherlands, so they are not illegal in our municipality either. This can prevent street dealing. The municipality can determine the locations and the number, giving it more control.

statements.title: The villages should receive their own budget that they may spend as they see fit.

titleParts.glossary: A sum of money for a specific task or assignment.

titleParts.text: krijgen dat ze naar eigen inzicht mogen besteden.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality sets various budgets, for example for greenery, street maintenance or a library. The municipality can choose to give each village its own budget: the residents can then decide themselves how to spend the money.
statements.pro: Residents themselves know best what their village and neighborhood need. With their own budget they can influence where the money goes and have more say over their living environment. This strengthens mutual connections.
statements.con: The municipal council ensures that villages get what they need. The council has already been elected by the residents. Providing structural funds for unclear purposes creates unnecessary separate pots of money. Villages can apply for subsidies and each village is treated equally.

statements.title: The municipality should provide shelter to refugees who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
statements.moreInfo: People can apply for asylum if they flee war or face danger in their own country. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) decides whether they may stay in the Netherlands. If that is not allowed, an asylum seeker must leave the country. They cannot always or do not want to return to their own country, but they no longer have a right to reception. The municipality can decide to offer this group basic accommodation (a place to sleep, food and a shower).
statements.pro: People, even without residency status, should not live on the street. Neighbors are affected as well. Humane shelter prevents these people from becoming homeless.
statements.con: Space and resources in the municipality are scarce. They should go to refugees who are legally allowed to remain here. The municipality cannot be obliged to shelter rejected asylum seekers. They must return to their country of origin.

statements.title: The municipality should offer people support to prevent divorces.
statements.moreInfo: Divorces have profound consequences for children, partners and for the demand for housing. In general the municipality keeps its distance from people’s personal relationships. The municipality can choose to try to prevent divorces by offering support.
statements.pro: Help to prevent a divorce can ensure children do not need youth care. If help can prevent a divorce, less extra housing is required. Supporting troubled relationships can prevent many social costs and future suffering.
statements.con: The municipality cannot prevent divorces and should not act as a marriage therapist; such assistance is too limited. Everyone, children and adults, should be safe at home. Those who need help will receive it. Support already exists for children affected by divorce.

statements.title: No new livestock farms should be allowed in Utrechtse Heuvelrug.

titleParts.text: In Utrechtse Heuvelrug mogen geen nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Farms where animals are kept, such as cows or pigs.

statements.moreInfo: Livestock farms provide food such as meat and dairy and create employment. They also produce nitrogen emissions. This has consequences for the health of nearby residents and for nature.
statements.pro: This is urgently needed to protect the natural environment and solve nitrogen problems. Farmers who want to work more sustainably can receive support. This prevents additional animal suffering and disease risks for animals and nearby residents.
statements.con: This is not the municipality’s responsibility. The national government and the province are the competent authorities and issue the permits for these businesses. Farmers should be able to decide what kind of business they run, within the rules.

statements.title: For new construction the municipality should allow apartment buildings of 9 floors instead of 6 as currently agreed.
statements.moreInfo: There is a housing shortage in the Netherlands and in the municipality. As a way to build more homes, the municipality can allow taller apartment buildings—nine storeys instead of six—even in the villages.
statements.pro: High-rise buildings can help solve the housing shortage while preserving the green character of the municipality. The current housing and care vision already allows nine storeys in special cases.
statements.con: Nine‑storey high‑rise buildings do not fit the village‑like, green character of our municipality. Nine‑storey buildings are not necessary to build enough housing for 52,000 inhabitants.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to capture or drive away wolves.
statements.moreInfo: On a walk in the forest you may encounter a wolf. Recently there have been incidents in which a wolf attacked people and livestock. The province is legally responsible for nature policy, and therefore also for wolf policy.
statements.pro: Measures are needed for the safety and welfare of our residents and their livestock. In case of nuisance there should be more options, such as capturing animals, relocating them to areas with sufficient space, or even shooting.
statements.con: The wolf is part of nature and contributes to biodiversity. The focus should be on education and prevention. Capturing or driving them away is not a solution. There is room for the wolf in the municipality, and owners should be helped to protect their livestock.

statements.title: For people with low incomes the municipality should pay the membership fees for sports clubs.
statements.moreInfo: For people with a low income, membership of a sports club is sometimes too expensive. The municipality may consider it important that they can also participate in sports and may pay their membership fees.
statements.pro: Every child and adult should be able to play sports, regardless of their financial situation. Sport is healthy, prevents loneliness and brings people in our villages together.
statements.con: There are already subsidies and other options, such as the Youth Sports Fund and community sports coaches. Sport is not a core municipal task.

statements.title: At Driebergen-Zeist station there should be more small-scale homes instead of offices and apartment buildings.

titleParts.glossary: Places where only a few people are housed together. Not many people in one place.

titleParts.text: woningen komen in plaats van kantoren en flatgebouwen.

statements.moreInfo: For the area around Driebergen-Zeist station a plan has been drawn up for new offices and 2,000 homes. The buildings would be taller than in many other parts of the municipality.
statements.pro: Small-scale housing fits the surroundings better. There is no great need for more commercial space at Driebergen-Zeist station.
statements.con: The municipality wants to build on as little land as possible and create more space for green areas and nature. Small‑scale housing actually requires more space.

statements.title: Solar panels should not be allowed on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: To supply everyone in the municipality with electricity, many new ways of generating power are needed. In the region Utrechtse Heuvelrug has agreed to contribute with clean energy, such as solar panels. The municipality can choose to use agricultural land for that purpose.
statements.pro: Agricultural land is needed for food production and nature and is scarce. There are plenty of other places for solar panels, such as roofs, facades, parking lots and infrastructure.
statements.con: Much more sustainable energy is needed quickly, for example through solar panels. Sometimes agricultural land must give way for that common good. Solar panels can very well coexist with other land uses, such as livestock, arable farming, or nature.

statements.title: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be allowed to wear a headscarf, yarmulke, or turban.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: ) moeten een hoofddoek, keppeltje of tulband kunnen dragen.

statements.moreInfo: The government is neutral. The Constitution guarantees freedom of religion and belief. BOAs are recognizable by their uniform and represent the municipality. Sometimes people want to express a belief through their clothing.
statements.pro: Enforcement officers employed by the government must behave neutrally and professionally. A religious expression does not stand in the way of that.
statements.con: Enforcement officers employed by the government must be neutral, and this should also be visible in their appearance.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.moreInfo: For vulnerable residents municipalities purchase care under the Youth Act and the Social Support Act (Wmo). In addition to legal requirements, the municipality can impose extra conditions on the organizations that provide the care.
statements.pro: Trust grows and care becomes more effective when you get a caregiver who suits you. Freedom of choice in care is important.
statements.con: Healthcare is already under great pressure and is the same for everyone regardless of origin, religion, or sexual orientation. The most important thing is that people receive timely care from respectful care providers.

statements.title: The municipality should allow permanent residence in holiday homes.
statements.moreInfo: There are many holiday parks with cottages within the municipality. Some of those cottages are used as permanent homes. In principle that is prohibited. The municipality may decide whether these cottages may be permanently inhabited.
statements.pro: All options are needed to counter the housing shortage. A recreation park can, for example, become a small neighborhood or hamlet. It is a solution for residents in need, such as after a separation, giving them a fixed address.
statements.con: This will lead to new recreational homes in other locations. The solution to the housing problem lies in building more homes. Holiday homes are needed to meet recreational demand; permanently living in a vacation cottage is not part of that.

statements.title: The municipality should oppose tall wind turbines at the provincial level.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich bij de provincie verzetten tegen hoge

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.moreInfo: To supply everyone in the municipality with electricity, many new ways of generating power are needed. In the region Utrechtse Heuvelrug has agreed to contribute with clean energy, such as wind turbines. The province decides where wind turbines will be placed. The average height of onshore wind turbines is 188 metres including the blades.
statements.pro: Large wind turbines damage the landscape and quality of life. They should be placed where no people live because they would be harmful to the health of nearby residents. Our municipality does not have such locations.
statements.con: All sources of clean energy are needed. Wind turbines contribute greatly. Wind energy is necessary for a sustainable future. The municipality should not oppose tall wind turbines on principle.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
statements.moreInfo: People with an income below 120% of the social assistance standard can currently receive financial help from the municipality. The municipality can choose to raise this income threshold and provide financial help to more people.
statements.pro: By raising that threshold, people who fall just above the minimum and cannot make ends meet can also receive support.
statements.con: For low-income residents the municipality already provides various additional services and assistance.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs).

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

statements.moreInfo: BOAs are employed by the municipality. They support the police and tackle nuisance and other disturbances in public spaces. The municipality can choose to hire more BOAs to reduce the workload of (neighbourhood) police officers.
statements.pro: More community enforcement officers improve enforcement and strengthen our residents' sense of safety.
statements.con: A clear division of tasks and cooperation with the police and other partners ensure a safe, livable neighborhood. More community enforcement officers (BOAs) are not necessary.

statements.title: In new housing developments 40% of the homes should be social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: There is a housing shortage in the Netherlands and in the municipality. The national standard for new construction is that 30% should be social rental housing. These are homes for people with low incomes, young people and starters. Utrechtse Heuvelrug can choose to raise that standard within its own municipality.
statements.pro: This is necessary to catch up on the backlog in our municipality. The housing shortage is too high. To keep housing affordable for low and middle incomes, 40% social rental housing is needed.
statements.con: Demand is highest for mid-rent housing. It is more important to build according to our residents’ needs than to meet this target percentage. To create mixed neighborhoods, especially other rental homes are needed in areas that already have many social housing units.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to create a direct bus line between Leersum and Amersfoort Central Station.
statements.moreInfo: The province is the authority responsible for regional bus services. The municipality can ask the province to introduce a direct bus line between Leersum and Amersfoort station. At present you have to change buses on that route.
statements.pro: Travel time will be significantly shorter, public transport will become a genuine alternative to the car, and villages and the city will be better connected.
statements.con: The current connection is adequate. In Doorn travelers can transfer.

statements.title: Utrechtse Heuvelrug should ban hunting on municipal land.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment anyone with a hunting licence may hunt wild animals within the municipality. The municipality can also prohibit that.
statements.pro: Recreational hunting is outdated. We must protect animals in our municipality, which is rich in nature. Hunting causes animal suffering and disturbs nature. Municipal land is usually near residential areas; hunting there can pose a danger or nuisance to people and pets.
statements.con: Hunting is necessary for proper wildlife management. A ban is not needed because the municipality does not have such a large territory and therefore little game. Forest management is outsourced. The organizations that manage these areas have their own policies.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to open the bus lane from Wijk bij Duurstede to Bunnik (the N229) to trucks.
statements.moreInfo: A lot of truck traffic currently goes through the villages. The municipality can ask the province to open the bus lane on the N229 to trucks. The province decides on regional roads.
statements.pro: This prevents freight traffic from driving through Doorn and the National Park.
statements.con: Truck traffic on the bus lane causes delays and unsafe conditions. The bus lane is intended for fast and reliable public transport.

statements.title: The municipality should create stricter rules on who qualifies for youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet strengere regels maken voor wie

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities are responsible for youth care, for example help with family problems and with the mental and behavioural problems of children and young people. The municipality is obliged to provide youth care, but can determine which type of care and for how long.
statements.pro: This prevents overlapping care. Stricter rules can reduce costs and can make youth care more effective. Much care can be provided just as well or better by the immediate environment.
statements.con: Stricter rules often lead to unnecessary bureaucracy. Stricter rules delay help. With stricter eligibility criteria we merely postpone young people’s problems. Costs should not be a reason to deny someone necessary care. Vulnerable children need the right care at the right time.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more streets where cars are "guests."
statements.moreInfo: With all road users (cars, cyclists, scooters and pedestrians) it is often busy on the streets. The municipality can choose to designate streets where the car is a guest: there cars may not drive fast and do not have priority.
statements.pro: This makes the streets safer, quieter, and more pleasant—important for a liveable public space. It encourages healthy activity on foot and by bike.
statements.con: It is not necessary. Streets where the speed limit is or will be 30 km/h are designed to match the traffic on that street. These roads are generally not safer for cyclists and can create dangerous situations. It is better to build proper cycle paths.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to allow new construction outside the village borders.
statements.moreInfo: New homes are needed in Utrechtse Heuvelrug. If the municipality wants to allow them to be built outside the village boundaries, the province must decide.
statements.pro: This is necessary to tackle the housing shortage. Filling all the empty spots in our villages is not a solution.
statements.con: Building outside village boundaries harms nature and the landscape.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no paid work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. The municipality may require them to do socially useful work, for example as extra help at a school. That may not be work that would normally be paid. The municipality may (temporarily) reduce the social assistance if someone refuses.
statements.pro: Meaningful community work as a reciprocal obligation is a step toward paid employment. Doing nothing and relying on handouts is not an option. Everyone can participate in society. Meaningful community work strengthens a person's self-worth and keeps people involved in the community.
statements.con: People on social assistance often have other problems. Extra requirements cause more stress and new problems. Mandatory obligations do not help people into sustainable work. It is better to encourage people without coercion to do (paid) work or other meaningful activities.

statements.title: The municipality should give residents with problematic debts a loan to pay off their debts.

titleParts.glossary: Large debts that someone can no longer repay on their own.

titleParts.text: een krediet geven waarmee ze hun schulden kunnen afbetalen.

statements.moreInfo: Large debts often lead to other problems, such as being unable to pay rent, having the electricity cut off, or becoming ill. Municipalities receive funds from the national government to help residents solve their debts. Municipalities decide for themselves how they provide help. This can also be a loan or credit with which someone repays all their debts.
statements.pro: A loan from the municipality helps people become debt-free faster. Credit provides peace of mind and clarity, and people have time to repay. Debts are often only one of the problems they face. A credit or loan helps people become more self-reliant.
statements.con: There is already sufficient policy for people with financial problems, such as statutory debt relief for individuals. Credit only makes spending on credit easier and the problems bigger. The municipality should not simply lend money to residents. Tailored solutions are better.

statements.title: The municipality should replace the two swimming pools in Driebergen and Doorn with one new swimming pool.
statements.moreInfo: You can swim in various places in the municipality, including two indoor swimming pools in Driebergen and Doorn. The municipality provides annual funding to a company to keep those pools open, clean, well maintained and safe. The municipality can choose to spend the money on one larger, more modern pool. For some residents that would then be further away.
statements.pro: These two swimming pools are outdated. It is better to replace them with one new, modern facility. Keeping two pools is not affordable. Our municipality already has relatively many pools for its population. A new pool would free up space for nature and housing where the two pools now stand.
statements.con: One central swimming pool is too large an investment. That money can be better used for more urgent problems. Every resident must be able to exercise, and the municipality must provide sufficient sports and play areas. This was proposed seven years ago and the plan was withdrawn after mass protests from clubs and residents.
